toolbox_gaps.summary: Need a reusable generic_visual_focus primitive for chartqa.

family_toolbox.name: chart_value_overlay
family_toolbox.applicability_conditions: This tool is applicable to images containing bar charts with numerical values overlaid or extractable via OCR. It is designed to work with charts where bars are visually distinct and can be detected using relative geometry. The chart should have a rectangular shape, and bars should be aligned horizontally or vertically. This tool does not handle charts with non-rectangular shapes or overlapping elements.
notes: Generates an overlay on a chart image to highlight bar-like regions and their approximate positions, aiding in manual value extraction.

coverage_contract.problem_pattern: Computing differences between chart values (e.g., largest vs. smallest bar)
supported_variations: Questions asking for differences between chart elements (e.g., bars, lines)
Charts with numerical values overlaid or extractable via OCR
unsupported_variations: Charts without numerical values or OCR-extractable data
Questions requiring non-numeric reasoning (e.g., qualitative comparisons)
forbidden_case_specific_assumptions: Hardcoding specific chart images or coordinates
Assuming a fixed chart type or layout
coverage_contract.why_this_should_generalize: The pattern involves a common chart analysis task (value comparison) that can be generalized across various chart types and questions.
